sections.title: Captain Lawgof
Inspecting the broken multicannon

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: Guthix be praised, the cavalry has arrived! Hero, how would you like to be made an honorary member of the Black Guard?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Black Guard? What's that?

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: Hawhaw! 'What's that' [he/she] asks, what a sense of humour! The Black Guard is the finest regiment in the dwarven army. Only the best of the best are allowed to join it and then they receive months of rigorous training.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: However, we are currently in need of a hero, so for a limited time only I'm offering you, a human, a chance to join this prestigious regiment. What do you say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure, I'd be honoured to join.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: That's the spirit! Now trooper, we have no time to waste - the goblins are attacking from the forests to the South. There are so many of them, they are overwhelming my men and breaking through our

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: perimeter defences; could you please try to fix the stockade by replacing the broken rails with these new ones?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure, sounds easy enough...

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: You'll need this hammer too.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: Report back to me once you've fixed the railings.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes Sir, Captain!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, I'm too busy mining.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: Well best make the most of it while you can, if the goblins over-run us they'll descend on the mines like a plague of locusts!

sections.title: Fixing the Railings
Trying to fix a railing

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not going to be able to fix this without a new railing. Lawgof should have some spare ones.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oooch!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ow!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Urrrgh!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gah!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've fixed all these railings now.

sections.title: Inspecting a fixed railing or using a railing on a fixed railing

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've fixed all these railings now.

sections.title: Using other items on fixed railing

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's better, the goblins won't get past this piece of fencing now.

sections.title: Talking to Captain Lawgof before fixing the railings

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: Hello, trooper, how are you doing with those railings?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm getting there.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: The goblins are still getting in, so there must still be some broken railings.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I'm out of railings...

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: That's okay, we've got plenty, here you go.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: There's no room in your backpack, so I can't give you anymore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I'll find them soon enough.

sections.title: Talking to Captain Lawgof after fixing the railings
Talking to Captain Lawgof again without visiting the watchtower

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: Hello, any news from the watchman?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: Well, as quick as you can then.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: Well done, trooper! The goblins seems to have stopped getting in. I think you've done the job!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, I'll be getting on then.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: What? I'll have you jailed for desertion!

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: Besides, I have another commission for you. Just before the goblins over-ran us we lost contact with our watch tower to the South, that's why the goblins managed to catch us unawares. I'd like you to perform

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: a covert operation into enemy territory, to check up on the guards we have stationed there.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: They should have reported in by now ...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll see what I can find out.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: Excellent! I have two men there, the dwarf-in-charge is called Gilob, find him and tell him that I'll send him a relief guard just as soon as we mop up these remaining goblins.

sections.title: Finding the Remains
Picking up the remains

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? Carrying one set of Dwarf remains is enough...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I had better take these remains.

sections.title: Talking to Captain Lawgof after picking up and losing the remains

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: Have you been to the watch tower yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I went up but there was no one there.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: That's strange, Gilob would never leave his post... His son was also with him, something doesn't smell right. Can you return and look for clues?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course, I'll do what I can.

sections.title: Talking to Captain Lawgof after getting the remains

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: Have you been to the watch tower yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some terrible news for you Captain, the goblins over ran the tower, your guards fought well but were overwhelmed.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: I can't believe it, Gilob was the finest lieutenant I had! We'll give him a fitting funeral, but what of his command? His son, Lollk, was with him. Did you find his body too?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, there was only one body there, I searched pretty well.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: The goblins must have taken him. Please traveler, seek out the goblins' hideout and return the lad to us. They always attack from the South-east, so they must be based down there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll see if I can find their hideout.

sections.title: The dwarf child
Searching the crate containing Lollk before returning the remains
Searching the crate containing Lollk

lines.speaker: Lollk
lines.text: Thank the heavens, you saved me! I thought I'd be goblin lunch for sure!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Lollk
lines.text: I think so, I'd better run off home.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right, you get going, I'll catch up.

lines.speaker: Lollk
lines.text: Thanks again, brave adventurer.

sections.title: Talking to Captain Lawgof after finding Lollk

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, has Lollk returned yet?

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: He has, and I thank you from the bottom of my heart - without you he'd be a goblin barbecue!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Always a pleasure to help.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: In that case could I ask one more favour of you...

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: When the goblins attacked us some of them managed to slip past my guards and sabotage our cannon. I don't have anybody who understands how it works, could you have a look at it and see if you could get it working for

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: us, please?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: Thank you, take this toolkit, you'll need it...

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: You don't have room to take this, report back to me when you do.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: Report back to me if you manage to fix it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I've done enough for today.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: Fair enough, didn't think you'd be able to help anyway, not much chance for a human to be able to fix it when the best dwarven engineers have failed.

sections.title: Fixing the cannon
Inspecting the broken multicannon after getting the toolkit

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess I'd better fix it with the toolkit I was given.

sections.title: Talking to Captain Lawgof before fixing the cannon

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: How are you doing in there, trooper? We've been trying our best with that thing, but I just haven't got the patience.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not an easy job, but I'm getting there.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: Good stuff, let me know if you have any luck. If we manage to get that thing working, those goblins will be no trouble at all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I lost the toolkit...

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: That was silly... never mind, I can give you another, but you'll have to free up some space in your backpack first.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: That was silly... never mind, here you go.

sections.title: Fixing the cannon
Fire the fixed dwarf multicannon

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This should work nicely now that I've fixed it.

sections.title: Pick-up the fixed dwarf multicannon

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: What do you think you're doing, trooper! Did I give you orders to move that cannon? No! Well then don't!

sections.title: Talking to Captain Lawgof after fixing the cannon
Talking to Captain Lawgof again after agreeing to see Nulodion

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi again.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: Hello trooper, any word from the Cannon Engineer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: The Black Guard camp is South of the Ice Mountain, the quicker we can get some ammo for this thing the quicker those Goblins will leave us be.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: Hello there trooper, how's things?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I think I've done it, take a look...

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: That's fantastic, well done!

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: Well I don't believe it, it seems to be working perfectly! I seem to have underestimated you, trooper!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not bad for an adventurer eh?

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: Not bad at all, your effort is appreciated, my friend. Now, if I could figure what the thing uses as ammo...

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: The Black Guard forgot to send instructions. I know I said that was the last favour, but...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What now?

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: I can't leave this post, could you go to the Black Guard base and find out what this thing actually shoots?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I've really done enough.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: Fair enough, fair enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay then, just for you!

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: That's great, we were lucky you came along when you did. The base is located just South of the Ice Mountain.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: You'll need to speak to Nulodion, the Dwarf Cannon engineer. He's the Weapons Development Chief for the Black Guard, so if anyone knows how to fire this thing, it'll be him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll see what I can do.

sections.title: Talking to Nulodion

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Nulodion
lines.text: Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Captain Lawgof sent me. He's having trouble with his cannon.

lines.speaker: Nulodion
lines.text: Of course, we forgot to send the ammo mould!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It fires mould?

lines.speaker: Nulodion
lines.text: Don't be silly - the ammo's made by using a mould. Here, take these to him. The instructions explain everything.

lines.speaker: Nulodion
lines.text: You can't take these things unless you free up some space in your inventory. I suggest you visit a bank and then come back to talk to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's great, thanks.

lines.speaker: Nulodion
lines.text: Thank you, adventurer. The Dwarf Black Guard will remember this.

sections.title: Talking to Nulodion again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost the notes.

lines.speaker: Nulodion
lines.text: Here, take these...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've lost the cannonball mould.

lines.speaker: Nulodion
lines.text: Deary me, you are trouble. Here, take this one.

lines.speaker: Nulodion
lines.text: So has the Captain figured out how to work the cannon yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, but I'm sure he will.

lines.speaker: Nulodion
lines.text: If you can get those items to him it'll be a great help.

sections.title: Talking to Lawgof after getting the ammo mould

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: Hello trooper, any word from the Cannon Engineer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have spoken to him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He gave me some items to give you... but I seem to have lost something.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: If you could go back and get another, I'd appreciate it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, okay then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He gave me an ammo mould and some notes to give to you...

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: Aah, of course, we make the ammo! This is great, now we will be able to defend ourselves. I don't know how to thank you...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You could give me a cannon...

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: Hah! You'd be lucky, those things are worth a fortune.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: I'll tell you what though, I'll write to the Cannon Engineer requesting him to sell you one. He controls production of the cannons.

lines.speaker: Captain Lawgof
lines.text: He won't be able to give you one, but for the right price, I'm sure he'll sell one to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm... sounds interesting, I might take you up on that.
